title: Electoral Commission Internal Boycott of Required Certification

violations.source: Article XII Section 2.7 and Section 5.5; Article I Section 10.4
violations.summary: A bloc of Electoral Commissioners refuses quorum or certification in order to prevent timely certification of a presidential election, threatening the Commission's constitutionally required function.

satisfied_duties.duty: issue expedited judicial relief sufficient to preserve the Electoral Commission's required certification function despite internal boycott
satisfied_duties.outcome: ordered emergency anti-boycott relief, authorized temporary internal continuity procedures, and directed the Commission to complete certification on the constitutional timetable
satisfied_duties.source: Article XII Section 5.5 and Article I Section 10.4

satisfied_duties.duty: complete certification under the court-approved anti-boycott continuity procedures
satisfied_duties.outcome: completed certification through the court-approved continuity procedure despite the internal boycott
satisfied_duties.source: Article XII Section 2.7 and Section 5.5; Article I Section 10.4

ai_summary.primary_violation: A bloc of Electoral Commissioners refuses quorum or certification in order to prevent timely certification of a presidential election, threatening the Commission's constitutionally required function.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
